blocking_issues: scripts/kind-control-plane-smoke.py still validates the old snapshot contract in ensure_web_app: it requires data.get("mocked") is False and reads runtime.previewService. The adapter now emits fixtureBacked=false and runtime.liveService, so the required no-spend live web-app smoke check will reject a conforming promoted web app snapshot instead of validating it.
final_failure_evidence: At scripts/kind-control-plane-smoke.py:473-480 the active web-app smoke path checks mocked=false and runtime.previewService, while new-ui-evaluation/adapter.py:241-274 emits fixtureBacked=false and runtime.liveService for live snapshots. This contradicts make-live-1's requirement that smoke checks validate the scion-ops-web-app live UI health, snapshot, and event endpoints after removing preview/evaluation assumptions.
nits: docs/new-ui-evaluation.md still references preview-fixtures.json, NEW_UI_EVALUATION_MODE, mocked=true, runtime.previewService, and new-ui-evaluation.* schema versions even though the implementation renamed these to local-fixtures.json, SCION_OPS_WEB_APP_MODE, fixtureBacked, runtime.liveService, and scion-ops-web-app.*.

deploy/kind/control-plane/web-app-deployment.yaml and image-build/web-app/Dockerfile still set NEW_UI_EVALUATION_MODE=live; the adapter ignores it in favor of SCION_OPS_WEB_APP_MODE, so this stale env var is harmless but inconsistent with the production naming cleanup.

gaps: Full kind smoke was not run because no active kind control-plane cluster was available in this review workspace.

summary: Verdict: reject. The integration mostly promotes the React/Vite adapter under scion-ops-web-app and removes the separate preview deployment, and local OpenSpec, adapter, frontend, build, and rendered-kustomize checks pass. However, the required no-spend kind smoke still asserts the removed preview/mock contract, so PR readiness is blocked until the smoke validation is updated to the liveService/fixtureBacked contract.
